text: Better not ruin my childhood memories by watching that again.  Saw a funny comment under some Dune themed video gen recently, "You realize all the characters in that scene would canonically murder you if they knew you used a computer to make it."

text:
meta note — this discussion has had a _lot_ of links pasted without any explanation or context. this has two issues:

1. i don't know what many of those links are about (eg: the HN and YT links). it'd be nicer for my experience reading your post if you had written a short description or copied the page title and made that the link. sometimes links will generate a rich preview at the bottom of the post, but those have bad UX problems so i usually remove them. can't rely on rich previews to add context.
2. if anyone ever tries to read this convo with a screen reader, they're going to have to listen to a lot of gobbledegook.
in the future, _please_ don't paste "naked" urls. write some text that _somehow_ suggests what the link is, and then make that text the link. thanks.

if anyone ever tries to read this convo with a screen reader, they're going to have to listen to a lot of gobbledegook.

text:
As I i can't edit my original message to this thread I want to put in here some context.

CONTEXT:

The Butlerian Jihad is a Conecpt from Dune: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dune:_The_Butlerian_Jihad>

It invokes for me the conecept of a quite drastical discourse with Technology, espedially AI.

elements.text:
As I i can't edit my original message to this thread I want to put in here some context.

CONTEXT:

The Butlerian Jihad is a Conecpt from Dune:
It invokes for me the conecept of a quite drastical discourse with Technology, espedially AI.

text:
In that sense I sent the first 4 Links in which we can see how AI companies try to avoid that their technology gets captured by war interests. But also Postmarket OS design rules in which they try to avoid AI consciously.

So as people try to define or find a - comfortable - position towards technology. I do find the notion of the Butlerian Jihad interesting. Can we reject AI? Can we reject Parts of AI? What will we do instead? And how do we start talking and thinking about this.

text: I'd like to normalize using <https://feelingof.com|inline links like this>, rather than posting bare URLs (ie where you see the http://yadda-yadda) — they're better for screen readers and generally more pleasant to look at, too. I think they also encourage better writing (but now we're getting into the weeds). So for future posts, I'd encourage you to add your links directly to the words that describe them.

text: Can we reject Parts of AI? — Mere months ago, I did not expect to find myself mostly lining up on the "burn it with fire" side, then again my feeble imagination absolutely failed to anticipate the abuses that I see daily.  It's bad friends.  So bad that when ranting to my sister the other night, she recognized drastic measures are in order: she recommended I find a venue that does open mic standup!  What is this world coming to?
